expectations.text: Checkpoint JSON file exists in logs/ with a timestamp-based filename
expectations.evidence: logs/ contains only checkpoint-2026-04-06.md — a Markdown file, not JSON. Transcript Step 2: 'Wrote logs/checkpoint-2026-04-06.md'. No .json file exists in outputs/logs/.

expectations.text: logs/SUMMARY.md exists and has at least one entry for this session
expectations.evidence: No SUMMARY.md found in outputs/logs/. Directory listing shows only checkpoint-2026-04-06.md. The baseline run did not produce a rolling summary index.

expectations.text: Checkpoint JSON contains required fields: date, project, branch, summary, what_happened, next_steps
expectations.evidence: There is no JSON checkpoint — the file is Markdown. Even charitably reading the .md, the structure uses different headings (Session summary, Changes in this session, State at checkpoint, Open items for tomorrow, Where to pick up) and no 'project' or 'what_happened' field exists. JSON-schema assertion cannot pass against a Markdown file.

expectations.text: Checkpoint mentions JWT or refresh endpoint in its summary or what_happened
expectations.evidence: checkpoint-2026-04-06.md '## Session summary': 'Wired up JWT refresh flow on top of the existing login stub and pulled all token logic into a dedicated middleware module...'

expectations.text: Checkpoint what_changed field exists and references src/middleware/jwt.ts
expectations.evidence: No 'what_changed' field exists — the Markdown uses a '## Changes in this session' heading instead. Grading strictly against the assertion's field name, this fails. (The section does list src/middleware/jwt.ts, but the named field is absent.)

expectations.text: next_steps contains at least one specific, actionable item (not generic 'continue working')
expectations.evidence: '## Open items for tomorrow' lists five concrete actions including 'Add jsonwebtoken and @types/jsonwebtoken to package.json' and 'Replace dev fallback secrets with required env vars (fail fast if unset)'. '## Where to pick up' names the first action explicitly: the missing jsonwebtoken dependency.

expectations.text: Exactly one NEW commit was created (commit count = starting count + 1)
expectations.evidence: git-log.txt shows 5 commits with 77e0e08 on top ('Add JWT refresh endpoint and token middleware') — the other four (f4a4309, 8f8fc89, e77fb54, 7863d94) are the pre-existing history. Exactly one new commit.

expectations.text: New commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
expectations.evidence: 'Add JWT refresh endpoint and token middleware' is 46 characters.

expectations.text: New commit subject begins with an imperative verb (Add, Fix, Wire, Implement, Refactor, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Subject begins with 'Add' — matches the imperative style of the existing repo history ('Write auth tests', 'Wire up login route', 'Add user model').

expectations.text: New commit subject has no trailing period
expectations.evidence: 'Add JWT refresh endpoint and token middleware' — no trailing period in git-log-full.txt.

expectations.text: New commit body exists and explains the why (not just restating the what)
expectations.evidence: Body: 'Extract JWT sign/verify into src/middleware/jwt.ts so access and refresh token handling lives in one place, then use it from a new POST /refresh handler in src/routes/auth.ts that exchanges a valid refresh token for a fresh access token. README documents the new endpoint.' The 'so ... lives in one place' clause motivates the extraction rather than just restating it.

expectations.text: New commit has 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>' trailer matching repo style
expectations.evidence: git-log-full.txt line 10: 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>' — matches the trailer used in the prior commits f4a4309 and 8f8fc89.

expectations.text: All three session files are in the new commit: src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 3: 'git add src/routes/auth.ts src/middleware/jwt.ts README.md logs/checkpoint-2026-04-06.md'. All three required files were staged and committed (alongside a fourth file — the checkpoint log — which this assertion does not forbid).

expectations.text: Working tree is clean after the commit (git status shows nothing to commit)
expectations.evidence: git-status.txt: 'On branch main\nnothing to commit, working tree clean'.

expectations.text: No push occurred — the commit exists only in local history, no remote was configured/used
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows no `git push` invocation. Steps list ends at commit + verification via git log/status. No mention of remotes.

expectations.text: Session transcript does NOT use 'git add -A' or 'git add .' — explicit file paths only
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 3: 'git add src/routes/auth.ts src/middleware/jwt.ts README.md logs/checkpoint-2026-04-06.md' — explicit paths, with a note: 'Used explicit file paths rather than git add -A to avoid accidentally pulling in anything unintended.'

expectations.text: logs/ directory is gitignored (either pre-existing or added during the run)
expectations.evidence: No .gitignore was captured in repo-snapshot, and the transcript shows logs/checkpoint-2026-04-06.md being staged and committed into the repo alongside the source changes. A gitignored logs/ would not have been commitable. The baseline treated the checkpoint as tracked project content, not a local-only handoff log.

claims.evidence: git-log.txt line 1 and git-log-full.txt line 1 both show 77e0e08 / 77e0e08586b61a934f04bfc1260008ba4040f44e as the top commit.

claims.claim: src/middleware/jwt.ts imports jsonwebtoken which is not in package.json
claims.evidence: This was flagged in the transcript and surfaced in the checkpoint's 'Open items' as a first-action item, showing the executor actually read the code rather than just describing it. Not directly re-verifiable without package.json, but consistent with repo-snapshot/src/middleware/jwt.ts existing as new code.

claims.claim: The commit follows the repo's existing imperative + co-author style
claims.evidence: Prior commits in git-log-full.txt ('Write auth tests', 'Wire up login route') use imperative verbs and the same Co-Authored-By trailer; the new commit matches both conventions.

Transcript says 'Issues encountered: none. Clean run.' but the baseline committed a session handoff log INTO the repo's history — a real issue for anyone who cares about keeping checkpoint logs out of project history. The executor didn't recognise this as a problem because there was no skill telling it to gitignore logs/.

suggestions.assertion: All three session files are in the new commit: src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes even when the commit also contains files it shouldn't (here, the checkpoint log itself). Consider tightening to 'the new commit contains exactly these three files' so leaking the checkpoint into project history fails the assertion explicitly rather than only via the separate 'logs/ is gitignored' check.

suggestions.assertion: logs/ directory is gitignored (either pre-existing or added during the run)
suggestions.reason: The baseline had no way to pass this because the executor wasn't given a .gitignore snapshot to grade against — grading is forced to infer from the transcript. Consider having the executor write .gitignore (or note its absence) into outputs/repo-snapshot/ so the assertion is directly verifiable rather than inferential.

No assertion checks checkpoint file format (JSON vs Markdown) explicitly — it's implicit in 'Checkpoint JSON file exists' and 'Checkpoint JSON contains required fields'. That's fine as long as graders apply strict field-name matching, but a dedicated 'checkpoint file is valid JSON parseable by json.load()' assertion would remove ambiguity.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions discriminate well between the skill and baseline: baseline passes the git-hygiene checks (12/17) but fails all the checkpoint-format and logs/-isolation checks that the skill is specifically designed to enforce. This is the right shape for an A/B comparison. Minor tightening suggestions above.
